agent_guidance: Prospect research kept clustering around missed follow-up, spreadsheet CRM use, and manual reminder pain.
profitability_note: This narrows the product to a recurring workflow that agencies and operators can justify paying for because it reduces missed revenue and admin overhead.
summary: Added the first CRM slice with an authenticated follow-up queue, overdue and due-today visibility, and a next-action surface for leads.
title: Lead follow-up workspace

agent_guidance: The agent was more useful when we constrained it to CRM-adjacent operator pain rather than generic startup ideas.
profitability_note: Better research targeting improves the odds that we build a narrow workflow with recurring demand instead of a vague all-in-one CRM.
summary: Tuned the prospect agent toward lead follow-up, pipeline hygiene, relationship memory, and token tracking so it can guide CRM scope instead of broad SaaS hunting.
title: CRM-direction prospecting profile

agent_guidance: The missing piece was not just more ideas, but a tighter loop between what the agent finds and what we actually ship.
profitability_note: A daily feedback loop keeps experimentation grounded in recurring pain and helps us avoid wandering into high-support, low-ROI features.
summary: Added a daily email briefing that summarizes prospect-agent guidance, tracked product updates, and whether the roadmap is converging on a profitable CRM wedge.
title: Daily operator briefing

agent_guidance: Repeated cooperative prospect runs kept reinforcing follow-up discipline, spreadsheet-held CRM workflows, and relationship memory as the highest-conviction CRM wedge.
profitability_note: This deepens daily recurring value in the narrowest useful workflow, which improves retention potential and helps us validate a follow-up-first CRM before expanding into broader CRM surface area.
summary: Added complete and snooze/reschedule actions to the CRM follow-up queue, exposed them through the FastAPI and Next.js API layers, and turned the /crm workspace into an interactive queue instead of a static view.
title: CRM follow-up actions

agent_guidance: The founder wanted every cooperative agent run to produce an immediate briefing and clarified that the Railway app key and editor key are intentionally different.
profitability_note: This tightens the feedback loop between research and product action while reducing credential ambiguity, which makes autonomous iteration more reliable and lowers the odds of debugging wasted on the wrong key path.
summary: Changed the operator briefing flow so each successful prospect-agent run sends its own briefing email, made the separate scheduled briefing job opt-in, and added an explicit APP_OPENAI_API_KEY path for app agents so local automation can use a dedicated credential separate from the editor environment.
title: Prospect briefings per run

agent_guidance: The founder wanted the prospect agent to see more of the open web so it can surface stronger workflow pain signals beyond the original social-source set.
profitability_note: A broader discovery surface should improve our odds of finding repeated, monetizable CRM workflow pain without depending on a single community, while still keeping the product direction grounded in narrow operational problems.
summary: Expanded the prospect agent with broad web search, Indie Hackers, and review-site discovery on top of the existing Reddit, Hacker News, X, and Discord sources, all through the shared public-search adapter and the existing CRM-direction filtering pipeline.
title: Broader prospect source bundle

agent_guidance: Recent cooperative runs kept reinforcing follow-up discipline plus fragmented client context, especially for solo consultants and small agencies juggling discovery notes, spreadsheets, and handoffs.
profitability_note: This deepens daily recurring value in the narrow wedge we care about by making the CRM useful as an operational memory system, which should improve retention and make the product harder to replace with generic reminders alone.
summary: Turned the CRM follow-up workspace into a relationship-memory surface by adding per-lead timeline history, latest-context display, and lightweight internal note capture on top of the existing queue actions.
title: CRM contact timeline and notes

agent_guidance: Cooperative runs kept reinforcing spreadsheet-friendly CRM depth, so /code now turns strong signals into explicit build briefs instead of vague idea dumps.
profitability_note: This shortens the loop between market signal detection and focused CRM execution without pretending the backend can safely self-edit production code.
summary: Added a Telegram /code command that runs the prospect agent, makes a build-or-hold decision, persists a structured autonomous build brief, and replies with the recommendation.
title: Add Telegram /code workflow

agent_guidance: The founder wanted Telegram to steer execution more directly, so /code guidance now overrides exploratory prioritization while still protecting the narrow CRM wedge.
profitability_note: This tightens the founder-to-execution loop and makes remote prioritization faster without letting hype or off-goal work derail the product.
summary: Updated /code so trailing text is parsed as explicit founder direction, passed into the cooperative prospect run, and queued unless it clearly conflicts with the profitable CRM goal.
title: Teach Telegram /code to accept founder guidance

agent_guidance: Live Railway logs showed /code reached the API but died on SMTP network failure before the useful Telegram outcome could land.
profitability_note: This keeps the founder feedback loop reliable under production infrastructure hiccups and prevents research/build automation from stalling on a secondary channel.
summary: Changed composite delivery to succeed when Telegram works even if SMTP fails, and made post-run operator briefings best-effort so /code and /prospect do not die on Railway email transport issues.
title: Make Telegram code runs survive SMTP failures

agent_guidance: The easiest honest bridge was to persist founder instructions on the server and let the local operator box poll them, rather than pretending Railway can message Codex directly.
profitability_note: This shortens the path from founder intent to local execution while keeping the research and build loop durable across deploys and restarts.
summary: Stored every Telegram /code request in Postgres, exposed an internal listing endpoint, and added a local automation sync job that can mirror those requests into a local inbox file on this always-on machine.
title: Add durable founder code request bridge

agent_guidance: The prospect agent kept reinforcing spreadsheet-held CRM workflows and onboarding friction, so we focused on making import safer and easier instead of adding heavy integrations first.
profitability_note: This reduces onboarding friction for spreadsheet-native teams, which should improve activation and make the follow-up-first CRM wedge easier to adopt without support-heavy setup.
summary: Added inline spreadsheet column mapping to the CRM import flow so users can correct ambiguous headers from the preview before import.
title: Guided CRM import mapping

agent_guidance: Prospect-guided CRM direction kept pointing toward spreadsheet-heavy onboarding, so this lays the groundwork for AI-assisted messy file and image interpretation while keeping advanced intake behind billing controls.
profitability_note: This creates a monetizable advanced-intake layer without forcing a heavyweight CRM rebuild and lets the product learn each account's recurring file formats over time.
summary: Added per-user CRM AI intake prompt and preferred import-format memory, persisted through account settings and surfaced in the CRM workspace with paid-only gating signals.
title: Add paid AI intake profile foundation

agent_guidance: The live production fault on /api/crm/import/preview showed spreadsheet intake must be resilient to real-world exports before we add more AI-driven intake on top.
profitability_note: Reliable spreadsheet onboarding protects activation at the top of the funnel and reduces support burden for the spreadsheet-friendly CRM wedge.
summary: Fixed CRM import preview and commit so messy CSV exports with problematic line endings no longer crash the backend; they now parse cleanly or return a user-facing validation error instead of a 500.
title: Harden CRM CSV import parsing

agent_guidance: The founder direction was to make messy client spreadsheets painless, and Excel support is a necessary part of that promise because many real operator workflows never export CSV first.
profitability_note: Supporting native Excel files removes a high-friction onboarding step, improving activation for spreadsheet-heavy teams without increasing long-term product complexity much.
summary: CRM import now accepts CSV, XLSX, and XLS uploads through the same preview and commit workflow, with server-side normalization into the existing spreadsheet validation pipeline.
title: Add Excel CRM import support

agent_guidance: Prospect runs kept reinforcing spreadsheet-friendly CRM workflows and pain around getting messy client data into the app quickly; the next best intake step was image capture plus a low-friction phone workflow.
profitability_note: This reduces onboarding friction, makes the spreadsheet-friendly wedge more practical in the field, and creates a clear paid AI feature with everyday operational value.
summary: Brivoly now accepts note images in the CRM import flow, supports paid AI interpretation of uploaded images, and lets users send note photos to their account through Telegram with a signed /intake caption.
title: Add AI note image capture for CRM

agent_guidance: Founder direction was to make image-ingestion routes configurable per user instead of assuming one path. This keeps intake flexible while the app is still greenfield.
profitability_note: This reduces onboarding friction for teams with different habits and makes the paid AI intake path easier to adopt without forcing a single send-in workflow.
summary: Account settings now store preferred image-intake channels and routing notes so each user can describe whether they expect to send note images by upload, Telegram, WhatsApp, email, or another workflow.
title: Add configurable CRM image intake channels
